cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if the sentence "Luke Voit was out at first." is plausible. Let me start by breaking down what this means. First, "Luke Voit" is a name. I believe he is a professional baseball player. I'll check my knowledge to confirm: Luke Voit is a first baseman who has played for several Major League Baseball (MLB) teams, including the New York Yankees. That makes sense. Next, the phrase "was out at first." is a baseball term. In baseball, when a batter hits the ball and reaches base, but is later tagged out or forced out at first base before advancing to second base, they are said to have been "out at first." This can happen in various situations, such as: 1. A ground ball is hit to the infield, and the batter fails to reach first base in time before the throw from the fielder.
